inventory.soc_percent stores last-known SOC; telemetry_events.soc_percent stores per-packet SOC. Reviewer must confirm both are needed.
products.voltage_v is nominal product voltage; telemetry_events.voltage_v is instantaneous reading. Distinct semantics but identical token set.
logical FK to inventory.serial_number; intentional same-name reuse

notes: BRD 6.2.4 mandates two new tables (telemetry_events, telemetry_daily_summary). No existing table holds per-packet IoT readings, so additive schema is justified.
BRD sync audit (line 487 of /tmp/nbfc_extract/sync_audit.txt) confirms 'IoT telemetry ingestion — Section 6.2 full spec — IN SYNC'.

AC2 specifies a unique constraint on (serial_number, summary_date) but telemetry_daily_summary.columns[] does not include the constraint; reviewer should ensure the migration adds .unique() or a composite uniqueIndex.

Fuzzy collisions are FK-style same-name reuse, not renames. Reviewer needs to either (a) accept the FK reuse pattern and approve schema, or (b) require renames to remove ambiguity. Strict ≥0.6 Jaccard rule produced these flags; boilerplate columns (id, created_at, updated_at) were excluded.
